- `vyre-core/src/dialect/workgroup/` (moves)

  `metadata.rs` (Agent A claims those; Agent C gets the rest)

- `vyre-core/src/cert.rs` + `vyre-core/src/cert/` (moves to conform)

- `vyre-core/src/test_migration.rs` (deletes)
- `vyre-core/src/bin/` (moves to xtask/)
- `vyre-core/src/fuzz/` (moves to a new `vyre-fuzz/` or deletes per LAW 9)
- `ops-facades/**` (deletes entirely)
- `vyre-primitives/` if it still exists (deletes; per uncommitted diff it's
  already being removed)
- `backends/photonic/` (deletes entirely)
- `conform/vyre-conform-spec/src/cert_support.rs` or equivalent (additions
  only — Agent C may add the cert logic here)

### Forbidden paths (Agent A or B owns these)

- `vyre-core/src/ir/**`, `vyre-core/src/optimizer/**`, `vyre-core/src/lower.rs`,
  `vyre-core/src/match_result.rs`, `vyre-core/src/ir.rs`, `vyre-core/src/optimizer.rs`

  `dialect.rs`, `enforce.rs`, `interner.rs`, `io.rs`, `lowering.rs`,
  `migration.rs`, `mutation.rs`, `op_def.rs`, `registry.rs`, `toml_loader.rs`
- `vyre-core/src/backend.rs` + `backend/`, `pipeline.rs`, `routing.rs` + `routing/`,
  `diagnostics.rs`, `error.rs` (Agent B)
- `vyre-wgpu/`, `backends/spirv/` (Agent B — renamed)
- `vyre-foundation/**`, `vyre-driver/**` (Agents A and B)
- `vyre-spec/**`, `vyre-macros/**`, `vyre-reference/**` (none)

### Shared files

- `vyre-core/src/lib.rs` — Agent C appends its re-exports, deletes
  `pub mod ops;`, `pub mod dialect;`, `pub mod cert;`, `pub mod routing;` (if not
  already claimed), `pub mod introspection;`, `pub mod test_migration;`.
- `vyre-core/src/dialect/mod.rs` — Agent C removes the feature-gated
  algorithm declarations (`#[cfg(feature = "dialect-buffer")] pub mod buffer;` etc.).
  Rebase-merge with Agent B who removes the scaffolding declarations.
- `vyre-core/Cargo.toml` — Agent C adds `vyre-ops = { workspace = true }`
  and removes the `[features]` `dialect-*` entries (no longer needed —
  feature gating happens in vyre-ops).
- `Cargo.toml` (workspace root) — Agent C removes the deleted members:
  `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-primitive"`, `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-hash"`,
  `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-string"`, `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-security"`,
  `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-compression"`, `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-graph"`,
  `"ops-facades/vyre-ops-workgroup"`, `"backends/photonic"`.

### Task C.3 — Recreate token_match_filter inside vyre-ops/pattern

Agent A deletes `vyre-core/src/ir/engine/token_match_filter.rs`. Recreate

test or consumer depends on `TokenType`. Imports from

### Task C.4 — Populate vyre-ops/src/lib.rs

```rust
//! vyre-ops — the standard op library for vyre.
//!
//! Feature-gated dialects. Each feature registers a set of OpDefs with
//! vyre-driver's DialectRegistry via `inventory::submit!`. Default
//! features include everything; fine-grained consumers opt in per module.

#[cfg(feature = "buffer")]           pub mod buffer;
#[cfg(feature = "decode")]           pub mod decode;
#[cfg(feature = "encode")]           pub mod encode;
#[cfg(feature = "hash")]             pub mod hash;
#[cfg(feature = "logical")]          pub mod logical;
#[cfg(feature = "math")]             pub mod math;
#[cfg(feature = "security")]         pub mod security;
#[cfg(feature = "stats")]            pub mod stats;
#[cfg(feature = "pattern")]          pub mod pattern;
#[cfg(feature = "string-similarity")] pub mod string_similarity;
#[cfg(feature = "wgsl-byte")]        pub mod wgsl_byte;
#[cfg(feature = "workgroup")]        pub mod workgroup;
#[cfg(feature = "primitive")]        pub mod primitive;
#[cfg(feature = "compression")]      pub mod compression;
#[cfg(feature = "rule")]              pub mod rule;
#[cfg(feature = "reductions")]       pub mod reductions;
#[cfg(feature = "data-movement")]    pub mod data_movement;
#[cfg(feature = "graph")]            pub mod graph;
#[cfg(feature = "crypto")]           pub mod crypto;
#[cfg(feature = "cooperative")]      pub mod cooperative;
#[cfg(feature = "string")]           pub mod string;
#[cfg(feature = "fixtures")]         pub mod fixtures;
#[cfg(feature = "sort")]             pub mod sort;
#[cfg(feature = "scan")]             pub mod scan;
#[cfg(feature = "match-ops")]        pub mod match_ops;
```

### Task C.9 — Edit vyre-core/src/ops.rs

The current file is an aggregator. After moves, every submodule it
declares is gone from vyre-core. Replace the entire file with:

```rust
//! Back-compat re-export shim for `vyre::ops::*`.
//!
//! Every op implementation now lives in the `vyre-ops` crate. This
//! module re-exports for downstream consumers that still use the
//! `vyre::ops::*` path. New code should depend on `vyre-ops` directly.

pub use vyre_ops::*;

// Spec-tier types stay in vyre-spec and are re-exported through
// vyre-foundation by Agent A.
pub use vyre_spec::{AlgebraicLaw, IntrinsicDescriptor};

// CPU op trait is foundation-tier.
pub use vyre_foundation::cpu_op::{CpuOp, CategoryAOp};
```

### Task C.10 — Edit vyre-core/src/dialect/mod.rs

After Agent B removes scaffolding and Agent C removes algorithm
declarations, the file should be replaced entirely with:

```rust
//! Back-compat re-export shim for `vyre::dialect::*`.
//!
//! Dialect scaffolding (registry, op_def, enforce, mutation, etc.)
//! lives in `vyre-driver`. Dialect algorithms (hash, pattern, crypto,
//! etc.) live in `vyre-ops`. This module re-exports both for legacy
//! callers.

pub use vyre_driver::registry::*;
pub use vyre_ops::*;
```

### Task C.13 — Compile gate

```
cargo build -p vyre-ops --no-default-features              # must succeed (empty build)
cargo build -p vyre-ops                                    # must succeed (all features)
cargo build -p vyre-ops --features hash,pattern            # must succeed (subset)
cargo build -p vyre --no-default-features                  # must succeed
cargo test -p vyre-ops                                     # must pass
cargo test -p vyre                                         # must pass at pre-restructure level
```

Dep audit: `cargo tree -p vyre-ops` must depend on
`vyre-foundation`, `vyre-driver`, `vyre-spec`, `vyre-macros` — and NOT
on `wgpu`, `naga`, or anything backend-specific. (Dialect algorithms
don't care which backend runs them; they register their OpDefs with
the registry and declarative lowerings use the driver's builder types.)

If `rmdir ops-facades/` fails because residual files exist (hidden
metadata, empty stub dirs from failed Agent 1 work), investigate first
— do not force-delete.

### Task C.17 — Consumer smoke test

Add `tests/consumer_smoke.rs` at workspace root proving the `vyre`
meta-crate shim still exposes every surface downstream consumers
(surgec, pyrograph, warpscan) depend on:

```rust
//! Meta-crate shim smoke test.
//!
//! Proves the post-restructure `vyre` facade still re-exports every
//! symbol downstream consumers use. If this compiles, no downstream
//! consumer broke.

use vyre::{
    Program, BufferDecl, DataType, Expr, Node,
    VyreBackend, BackendError, DispatchConfig, CompiledPipeline,
    Memory, MemoryRef,
    validate, ValidationError,
    AlgebraicLaw,
    BinOp, UnOp, AtomicOp, BufferAccess, OpId,
    CacheLocality, MemoryHints, MemoryKind,
    ArenaProgram, ExprArena, ExprRef, ExprNode, Ident,
    OpSignature, Convention, DataTypeSizeBytes,
    inline_calls, inline_calls_with_resolver, OpResolver,
    cse, dce, optimize,
    LimitState, DEFAULT_MAX_CALL_DEPTH, DEFAULT_MAX_NESTING_DEPTH,
    DEFAULT_MAX_NODE_COUNT,
    MemoryOrdering,
};

// Nested module re-exports (the back-compat paths)
use vyre::ir;
use vyre::ops;
use vyre::dialect;
use vyre::backend;
use vyre::pipeline;
use vyre::routing;
use vyre::diagnostics;
use vyre::error;
use vyre::lower;

#[test]
fn facade_symbols_resolve() {
    // Type existence check — each `size_of` forces the compiler to
    // resolve the full type path through the meta-crate shim.
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<Program>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<Expr>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<Node>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<DataType>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<BinOp>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<UnOp>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<AtomicOp>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<DispatchConfig>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<BackendError>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<ValidationError>();
    let _ = std::mem::size_of::<AlgebraicLaw>();
}

#[test]
fn facade_builds_a_program() {
    let _ = Program::new(
        vec![BufferDecl::output("out", 0, DataType::U32).with_count(1)],
        [1, 1, 1],
        vec![Node::Return],
    );
}
```

Add to `Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[test]]
name = "consumer_smoke"
path = "tests/consumer_smoke.rs"
```

Acceptance: `cargo test --test consumer_smoke` passes.

### Task C.18 — Fix dialect lowerings that use raw WGSL string parsing

Per `scripts/check_no_string_wgsl.sh`, dialect algorithm subtrees
contain sites where WGSL is built by string concatenation and then
fed to `naga::front::wgsl::parse_str`. Specifically:

- `vyre-ops/src/workgroup/queue_priority/lowering.rs:41-43`
- `vyre-ops/src/workgroup/stack/lowering.rs:43-44`
- All `vyre-ops/src/security/*/wgsl.rs` sites using
  `naga::front::wgsl::parse_str(wgsl).expect("valid wgsl")` — audit with:
  ```
  grep -rn "naga::front::wgsl::parse_str" vyre-ops/src/ | wc -l
  ```

For each site:
- Replace the `format!(wgsl) + parse_str` pair with calls into the
  `NagaBuilder` trait (lives in `vyre-driver` per Agent B's work).
- Delete every `.expect("valid wgsl")` — this is LAW 9 (documented
  panic vector).

This is a large change across many files. If full Law-B closure cannot
land in the v0.6 window, Agent C scopes to:
1. Move all sites into `vyre-ops/` (that part is mechanical — happens
   naturally during Task C.1/C.2 moves).
2. Replace every `.expect("valid wgsl")` with a `BackendError` return.
3. Add each remaining string-WGSL site to `.internals/audits/law_b_remaining.md`
   with filename, line, owning dialect, and the specific naga::Module
   shape it needs to become. File is committed. Remaining sites are
   work for a single follow-up commit that Omega tracks; no new sites
   are introduced.

Acceptance: zero `.expect("valid wgsl")` anywhere in `vyre-ops/src/`.
`scripts/check_no_string_wgsl.sh` count is strictly lower than
pre-restructure baseline.

### Task C.19 — No TODOs, no stubs, no evasion

Same rules as Agents A and B. If a dialect op tested successfully
before the move, it tests successfully after. No weakened assertions,
no `#[ignore]` added, no "known limitation" comments inserted.

## Commit message

```
vyre §C: extract vyre-ops crate, consolidate dialect + ops trees

Moves all 12 dialect algorithm subtrees and all 22 ops subtrees into a
new vyre-ops crate with per-domain cargo features. Merges overlap
between dialect/ and ops/ (the two trees claiming the same
responsibility) into single module paths.

Moves cert machinery to vyre-conform-spec, introspection + bin to
xtask, deletes test_migration and the ops-facades wrapper crates
(7 crates, all thin re-exports), deletes backends/photonic (never
shipped).

Consolidates the dialect-* Cargo features from vyre-core into
vyre-ops's own feature set. vyre-core re-exports everything moved so
`vyre::ops::*` and `vyre::dialect::*` continue to resolve.

Part of the v0.6 restructure per .internals/planning/COMPUTE_2_0.md.
```

## Done when

1. `vyre-ops` crate exists with 24+ feature-gated modules populated.
2. `cargo build -p vyre-ops` succeeds with default features.
3. `cargo build -p vyre-ops --no-default-features` succeeds (zero modules).
4. `cargo test -p vyre-ops` passes.
5. `cargo build -p vyre` succeeds with all re-exports resolving.
6. `vyre-core/src/dialect/` contains only `mod.rs` (a shim).
7. `vyre-core/src/ops/` contains only `ops.rs` (a shim) — Agent A's
   `cpu_op.rs`, `cpu_references.rs`, and `metadata.rs` have moved to
   vyre-foundation so the `ops/` directory should be empty.
8. `ops-facades/` no longer exists.
9. `backends/photonic/` no longer exists.
10. `vyre-core/src/test_migration.rs` no longer exists.
11. `vyre-core/src/cert.rs` + `cert/` no longer exist.
12. `vyre-core/src/introspection.rs` moved to `xtask/src/`.
13. `vyre-core/src/bin/` moved to `xtask/src/bin/`.
14. Workspace `Cargo.toml` members list no longer includes deleted crates.
15. `ops-facades/` container directory removed.
16. Root-level scratch files moved to `.internals/scratch/` (scratch.rs,
    build_tree.py, migrate_inventory*.py, test_if.wgsl).
17. Agent 1 debug leftovers deleted (check.json, check.txt, errors.txt,
    my_good_changes.patch, match_inspector.py, split_naga*.py,
    strip*.py, test_regex.py, vyre-core/strip*.py).
18. `vyre-reference/src/primitive/` consolidated into `primitives/`.
19. `tests/consumer_smoke.rs` exists and passes — proves meta-crate
    shim covers every downstream surface.
20. Zero `.expect("valid wgsl")` in `vyre-ops/src/`; remaining
    string-WGSL sites (if any) catalogued in
    `.internals/audits/law_b_remaining.md` with concrete migration targets.
21. `scripts/check_no_string_wgsl.sh` count strictly lower than
    pre-restructure baseline.
22. Single commit pushed to main.
